NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. With stringed instruments. On an eight-stringed harp. A Psalm of David. O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your anger, Nor chasten me in Your hot displeasure.
NKJV.2: Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I am weak; O LORD, heal me, for my bones are troubled.
NKJV.3: My soul also is greatly troubled; But You, O LORD—how long?
NKJV.4: Return, O LORD, deliver me! Oh, save me for Your mercies’ sake!
NKJV.5: For in death there is no remembrance of You; In the grave who will give You thanks?
NKJV.6: I am weary with my groaning; All night I make my bed swim; I drench my couch with my tears.
NKJV.7: My eye wastes away because of grief; It grows old because of all my enemies.
NKJV.8: Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity; For the LORD has heard the voice of my weeping.
NKJV.9: The LORD has heard my supplication; The LORD will receive my prayer.
NKJV.10: Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly troubled; Let them turn back and be ashamed suddenly.

ESV.1: O LORD, rebuke me not in your anger, nor discipline me in your wrath.
ESV.2: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am languishing; heal me, O LORD, for my bones are troubled.
ESV.3: My soul also is greatly troubled. But you, O LORD — how long?
ESV.4: Turn, O LORD, deliver my life; save me for the sake of your steadfast love.
ESV.5: For in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol who will give you praise?
ESV.6: I am weary with my moaning; every night I flood my bed with tears; I drench my couch with my weeping.
ESV.7: My eye wastes away because of grief; it grows weak because of all my foes.
ESV.8: Depart from me, all you workers of evil, for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
ESV.9: The LORD has heard my plea; the LORD accepts my prayer.
ESV.10: All my enemies shall be ashamed and greatly troubled; they shall turn back and be put to shame in a moment.

NASB.1: For the choir director; with stringed instruments, upon an eight-string lyre. A Psalm of David. O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your anger, Nor chasten me in Your wrath.
NASB.2: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I [am] pining away; Heal me, O LORD, for my bones are dismayed.
NASB.3: And my soul is greatly dismayed; But You, O LORD-- how long?
NASB.4: Return, O LORD, rescue my soul; Save me because of Your lovingkindness.
NASB.5: For there is no mention of You in death; In Sheol who will give You thanks?
NASB.6: I am weary with my sighing; Every night I make my bed swim, I dissolve my couch with my tears.
NASB.7: My eye has wasted away with grief; It has become old because of all my adversaries.
NASB.8: Depart from me, all you who do iniquity, For the LORD has heard the voice of my weeping.
NASB.9: The LORD has heard my supplication, The LORD receives my prayer.
NASB.10: All my enemies will be ashamed and greatly dismayed; They shall turn back, they will suddenly be ashamed.

NLT.1:
O LORD, don’t rebuke me in your anger
or discipline me in your rage.
NLT.2:
Have compassion on me, LORD, for I am weak.
Heal me, LORD, for my bones are in agony.
NLT.3:
I am sick at heart.
How long, O LORD, until you restore me?
NLT.4:
Return, O LORD, and rescue me.
Save me because of your unfailing love.
NLT.5:
For the dead do not remember you.
Who can praise you from the grave?
NLT.6:
I am worn out from sobbing.
All night I flood my bed with weeping,
drenching it with my tears.
NLT.7:
My vision is blurred by grief;
my eyes are worn out because of all my enemies.
NLT.8:
Go away, all you who do evil,
for the LORD has heard my weeping.
NLT.9:
The LORD has heard my plea;
the LORD will answer my prayer.
NLT.10:
May all my enemies be disgraced and terrified.
May they suddenly turn back in shame.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments, upon the eight-stringed lyre. A Psalm by David. Yahweh, don’t rebuke me in your anger, neither discipline me in your wrath.
WEB.2: Have mercy on me, Yahweh, for I am faint. Yahweh, heal me, for my bones are troubled.
WEB.3: My soul is also in great anguish. But you, Yahweh — how long?
WEB.4: Return, Yahweh. Deliver my soul, and save me for your loving kindness’ sake.
WEB.5: For in death there is no memory of you. In Sheol,  who shall give you thanks?
WEB.6: I am weary with my groaning. Every night I flood my bed. I drench my couch with my tears.
WEB.7: My eye wastes away because of grief. It grows old because of all my adversaries.
WEB.8: Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity, for Yahweh has heard the voice of my weeping.
WEB.9: Yahweh has heard my supplication. Yahweh accepts my prayer.
WEB.10: May all my enemies be ashamed and dismayed. They shall turn back, they shall be disgraced suddenly.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments, set [possibly] an octave below. A Psalm of David. O LORD, do not rebuke or punish me in Your anger,
Nor discipline me in Your wrath.
AMP.2:
Have mercy on me and be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am weak (faint, frail);
Heal me, O LORD, for my bones are dismayed and anguished.
AMP.3:
My soul [as well as my body] is greatly dismayed.
But as for You, O LORD—how long [until You act on my behalf]?
AMP.4:
Return, O LORD, rescue my soul;
Save me because of Your [unfailing] steadfast love and mercy.
AMP.5:
For in death there is no mention of You;
In Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) who will praise You and give You thanks?
AMP.6:
I am weary with my groaning;
Every night I soak my bed with tears,
I drench my couch with my weeping.
AMP.7:
My eye grows dim with grief;
It grows old because of all my enemies.
AMP.8:
Depart from me, all you who do evil,
For the LORD has heard the voice of my weeping.
AMP.9:
The LORD has heard my supplication [my plea for grace];
The LORD receives my prayer.
AMP.10:
Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly horrified;
Let them turn back, let them suddenly be ashamed [of what they have done].

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Please, God, no more yelling,
no more trips to the woodshed.
MSG.2:
Treat me nice for a change;
I'm so starved for affection.
Can't you see I'm black and blue,
beat up badly in bones
MSG.3:
and soul?
God, how long will it take
for you to let up?
MSG.4:
Break in, God, and break up this fight;
if you love me at all, get me out of here.
MSG.5:
I'm no good to you dead, am I?
I can't sing in your choir if I'm buried in some tomb!
MSG.6:
I'm tired of all this—so tired. My bed
has been floating forty days and nights
On the flood of my tears.
My mattress is soaked, soggy with tears.
MSG.7:
The sockets of my eyes are black holes;
nearly blind, I squint and grope.
MSG.8:
Get out of here, you Devil's crew:
at last God has heard my sobs.
MSG.9:
My requests have all been granted,
my prayers are answered.
MSG.10:
Cowards, my enemies disappear.
Disgraced, they turn tail and run.

NIV.1:
Psalm 6
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. According to sheminith. A psalm of David.
O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger
or discipline me in your wrath.
NIV.2:
Be merciful to me, Lord, for I am faint;
O Lord, heal me, for my bones are in agony.
NIV.3:
My soul is in anguish.
How long, O Lord, how long?
NIV.4:
Turn, O Lord, and deliver me;
save me because of your unfailing love.
NIV.5:
No one remembers you when he is dead.
Who praises you from the grave?
NIV.6:
I am worn out from groaning;
all night long I flood my bed with weeping
and drench my couch with tears.
NIV.7:
My eyes grow weak with sorrow;
they fail because of all my foes.
NIV.8:
Away from me, all you who do evil,
for the Lord has heard my weeping.
NIV.9:
The Lord has heard my cry for mercy;
the Lord accepts my prayer.
NIV.10:
All my enemies will be ashamed and dismayed;
they will turn back in sudden disgrace.

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger,
or discipline me in your wrath.
NRSVCE.2:
Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am languishing;
O LORD, heal me, for my bones are shaking with terror.
NRSVCE.3:
My soul also is struck with terror,
while you, O LORD — how long?
NRSVCE.4:
Turn, O LORD, save my life;
deliver me for the sake of your steadfast love.
NRSVCE.5:
For in death there is no remembrance of you;
in Sheol who can give you praise?
NRSVCE.6:
I am weary with my moaning;
every night I flood my bed with tears;
I drench my couch with my weeping.
NRSVCE.7:
My eyes waste away because of grief;
they grow weak because of all my foes.
NRSVCE.8:
Depart from me, all you workers of evil,
for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
NRSVCE.9:
The LORD has heard my supplication;
the LORD accepts my prayer.
NRSVCE.10:
All my enemies shall be ashamed and struck with terror;
they shall turn back, and in a moment be put to shame.

NET.1: LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger! Do not discipline me in your raging fury!
NET.2: Have mercy on me, LORD, for I am frail! Heal me, LORD, for my bones are shaking!
NET.3: I am absolutely terrified, and you, LORD — how long will this continue?
NET.4: Relent, LORD, rescue me! Deliver me because of your faithfulness!
NET.5: For no one remembers you in the realm of death, In Sheol who gives you thanks?
NET.6: I am exhausted as I groan; all night long I drench my bed in tears; my tears saturate the cushion beneath me.
NET.7: My eyes grow dim from suffering; they grow weak because of all my enemies.
NET.8: Turn back from me, all you who behave wickedly, for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping!
NET.9: The LORD has heard my appeal for mercy; the LORD has accepted my prayer.
NET.10: May all my enemies be humiliated and absolutely terrified! May they turn back and be suddenly humiliated!
